intro: Ceropegia ballyi, also known as Bally's rope vine, is a succulent plant species endemic to the tropical regions of Western Ghats in India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia ballyi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ceropegia ballyi is commonly used to prepare various herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
